_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to recognise and name every letter of the alphabet — both upper-case and lower-case — and know the sounds they make.

_source.w: We are learning to recognise and name every letter of the alphabet — both upper-case and lower-case — and know the sounds they make.
y: Knowing all the letters and their sounds gives us the tools we need to read any word.
Letter knowledge is the alphabet toolbox that we use every time we read or write.
The faster we can recognise letters and sounds, the more fluent our reading will become.
eg: We recognise the letters in our own name and find them in words around the classroom.
We practise naming letters quickly using flashcards or letter mats.
We sing the alphabet song and point to each letter as we go.
We sort letters into upper-case and lower-case pairs.
We play a letter hunt — finding specific letters hidden around the room.
We match each letter to a picture of something that starts with its sound — like 'b' with 'ball'.
